name: National Police
description_short: National Police Association is a 501(c)(3 non-profit advocacy organization supporting law enforcement through policy, legal defense, and public education—not a consumer finance company.
description_long: The National Police Association (NPA) is a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization founded to support law enforcement agencies and officers through education, advocacy, and legal defense. The organization operates entirely outside the consumer finance space and provides no lending, credit, or financial services of any kind. Instead, it focuses on advancing pro-law-enforcement messaging and policy at federal and local levels. The NPA's five core missions include policy advocacy around crime-fighting legislation, legal defense of law enforcement in court, support tools for local police departments, petition campaigns for quality-of-life policing, and free educational resources. Their services include filing lawsuits and FOIA requests on behalf of law enforcement, entering cases as amicus curiae (friend of the court), producing educational materials like podcasts and free courses, managing media campaigns through billboards and PSAs, and organizing advocacy petitions. What distinguishes the NPA is its exclusively pro-law-enforcement focus and its nonprofit status, meaning it operates under tax-exempt rules and reinvests all revenue into its stated missions rather than shareholder returns. The organization provides no consumer financial products, credit services, emergency loans, or lending of any kind. For consumers searching CreditDoc for actual financial assistance, the NPA is entirely irrelevant—it serves law enforcement agencies and their supporters, not people seeking credit, loans, or financial counseling.
services: Legal defense and filing of lawsuits on behalf of law enforcement

Policy review and legislative advocacy at federal and local levels

Mobile and static billboard campaigns with #SupportThePolice messaging
Public Service Announcements (PSAs) and media outreach

Reward program for law enforcement supporters
Thank you card and recognition programs for officers

Offers completely free educational resources including audio books, e-books, and podcasts
Provides legal backing and court defense specifically for law enforcement agencies
Operates a rewards program and support initiatives for police officers
Conducts public advocacy and outreach on legislation affecting public safety
